64ab6873f814f28d9b5a7848.question: This playwright created the character of Foka, who gets a year reduced from his sentence for every criminal he hangs. This playwright called for moving walls in order to reduce the sizes of locations for his dramatic adaptation of The Possessed. This author’s first play depicts Caligula orchestrating his own death. At the end of a play by this author, the Old Man refuses help to Maria, who prays that she will be turned into stone. The protagonist of a play by this author refuses to admit to murder in order to save his own life after a proposition by the Grand Duchess. In a play by this author, Martha and her mother accidentally murder their family member Jan, causing them both to commit suicide. A play by this author focuses on the revolutionary (*) Ivan Kaliayev’s assassination of Sergei Alexandrovich. For 10 points, name this French playwright of The Just Assassins and The Misunderstanding, which was referenced in a newspaper clipping that the protagonist reads in this author’s novel The Stranger.
64ab6873f814f28d9b5a7848.question_sanitized: This playwright created the character of Foka, who gets a year reduced from his sentence for every criminal he hangs. This playwright called for moving walls in order to reduce the sizes of locations for his dramatic adaptation of The Possessed. This author's first play depicts Caligula orchestrating his own death. At the end of a play by this author, the Old Man refuses help to Maria, who prays that she will be turned into stone. The protagonist of a play by this author refuses to admit to murder in order to save his own life after a proposition by the Grand Duchess. In a play by this author, Martha and her mother accidentally murder their family member Jan, causing them both to commit suicide. A play by this author focuses on the revolutionary (*) Ivan Kaliayev's assassination of Sergei Alexandrovich. For 10 points, name this French playwright of The Just Assassins and The Misunderstanding, which was referenced in a newspaper clipping that the protagonist reads in this author's novel The Stranger.

64ab6873f814f28d9b5a784d.question: A character in a play by this author hopes for a wheat shortage in Ukraine so that she can make enough money to afford a new dress. Mr. Mell and the police officer Dwornitschek stage a rehearsal in a play by this author that Tom Stoppard adapted into his play Rough Crossing. The seven children of Marie and Wolf Beifeld never appear on stage in a play by this author. In a play by this author, the Devil disguises himself as Doctor Miller to interfere with the painter Karl Mahler’s love life. Sandor Turai creates a fake play so that Albert Adam will think that Almady and Ilona Szabo were rehearsing lines in this author’s Play at the Castle. (*) Ficsur convinces the protagonist of a play by this author to commit a crime in order to avoid returning to the jealous Mrs. Muskat. In that play by this author, the title character kills himself during a robbery, but is later allowed one day to help his daughter Louise in order to get into Heaven. For 10 points, name this Hungarian author whose play Liliom was the basis for the musical Carousel.
64ab6873f814f28d9b5a784d.question_sanitized: A character in a play by this author hopes for a wheat shortage in Ukraine so that she can make enough money to afford a new dress. Mr. Mell and the police officer Dwornitschek stage a rehearsal in a play by this author that Tom Stoppard adapted into his play Rough Crossing. The seven children of Marie and Wolf Beifeld never appear on stage in a play by this author. In a play by this author, the Devil disguises himself as Doctor Miller to interfere with the painter Karl Mahler's love life. Sandor Turai creates a fake play so that Albert Adam will think that Almady and Ilona Szabo were rehearsing lines in this author's Play at the Castle. (*) Ficsur convinces the protagonist of a play by this author to commit a crime in order to avoid returning to the jealous Mrs. Muskat. In that play by this author, the title character kills himself during a robbery, but is later allowed one day to help his daughter Louise in order to get into Heaven. For 10 points, name this Hungarian author whose play Liliom was the basis for the musical Carousel.
64ab6873f814f28d9b5a784d.answer: Ferenc Molnár (the first and fourth lines refer to The Devil)
64ab6873f814f28d9b5a784d.answer_sanitized: Ferenc Molnar (the first and fourth lines refer to The Devil)

64ab6873f814f28d9b5a7853.question: Author and type of play required. In one of these plays by this author, the protagonist throws off his coat before asking the audience to tell another character that “her scarecrow husband… behaved with dignity.” In one of these plays by this author, a character complains about his sister-in-law asking for a copper jar and a “lamp-globe” in a long monologue. A character repeatedly tells her servant to give Toby an extra-share of oats in one of these plays by this author. In one of these plays by this author, Tolkachov goes on a rant about his miserable life after asking for a revolver and begins screaming “I want blood!” Nyukhin is forced to give the title boring lecture in one of these plays by this author called On the (*) Harmful Effects of Tobacco. In one of these plays by this author, Smirnov confesses his love for Popova after coming to collect debts from her late husband. The Tragedian in Spite of Himself and The Bear are examples of these plays by this author. For 10 points, name these characteristically short plays by the author of The Cherry Orchard.
64ab6873f814f28d9b5a7853.question_sanitized: Author and type of play required. In one of these plays by this author, the protagonist throws off his coat before asking the audience to tell another character that "her scarecrow husband... behaved with dignity." In one of these plays by this author, a character complains about his sister-in-law asking for a copper jar and a "lamp-globe" in a long monologue. A character repeatedly tells her servant to give Toby an extra-share of oats in one of these plays by this author. In one of these plays by this author, Tolkachov goes on a rant about his miserable life after asking for a revolver and begins screaming "I want blood!" Nyukhin is forced to give the title boring lecture in one of these plays by this author called On the (*) Harmful Effects of Tobacco. In one of these plays by this author, Smirnov confesses his love for Popova after coming to collect debts from her late husband. The Tragedian in Spite of Himself and The Bear are examples of these plays by this author. For 10 points, name these characteristically short plays by the author of The Cherry Orchard.
64ab6873f814f28d9b5a7853.answer: one-act plays by Anton Chekhov [prompt on short plays by Anton Chekhov]

64ab6873f814f28d9b5a7855.question: In the essay “Theater of the Masses,” a critic of this conflict identified commercial interests, old realists, and young practitioners of poetic theater as the three “minorities” of drama during this conflict. James McCarthy analyzed two lost plays written during this conflict that he termed “militant marionettes.” In a play set during this conflict, a boy is killed while fishing because of his shabby cap and the title character keeps bread baking in the oven throughout the play. The comedic play Four Hearts with Brake and Reverse was premiered during this conflict. After participating in this conflict, George Leeson wrote the play (*) This Trampled Earth about this conflict. It’s not the Thirty Years’ War, but Helene Weigel played the title character in a Bertolt Brecht play titled for her “rifles” set during this conflict. Simple and straightforward plays produced during this conflict were referred to as “urgent theater.” Max Aub and Rafael Alberti were exiled following this conflict. For 10 points, Federico Garcia Lorca was assassinated during what conflict?
64ab6873f814f28d9b5a7855.question_sanitized: In the essay "Theater of the Masses," a critic of this conflict identified commercial interests, old realists, and young practitioners of poetic theater as the three "minorities" of drama during this conflict. James McCarthy analyzed two lost plays written during this conflict that he termed "militant marionettes." In a play set during this conflict, a boy is killed while fishing because of his shabby cap and the title character keeps bread baking in the oven throughout the play. The comedic play Four Hearts with Brake and Reverse was premiered during this conflict. After participating in this conflict, George Leeson wrote the play (*) This Trampled Earth about this conflict. It's not the Thirty Years' War, but Helene Weigel played the title character in a Bertolt Brecht play titled for her "rifles" set during this conflict. Simple and straightforward plays produced during this conflict were referred to as "urgent theater." Max Aub and Rafael Alberti were exiled following this conflict. For 10 points, Federico Garcia Lorca was assassinated during what conflict?
64ab6873f814f28d9b5a7855.answer: Spanish Civil War (Four Hearts with Break and Reverse is by Enrique Jardiel Poncela)
64ab6873f814f28d9b5a7855.answer_sanitized: Spanish Civil War (Four Hearts with Break and Reverse is by Enrique Jardiel Poncela)

64ab6873f814f28d9b5a7858.question: In one play, this character decides upon a change in profession after proclaiming that “a shoe maker in making shoes cannot spoil a scrap of leather without paying for it.” After four doctors cannot decide on the best treatment for this character’s daughter, he attempts to search for a panacea called Orvietan. This character is ordered by his master to invite a stone statue of “the Commander” in the forest to a dinner. In one play, this character accidentally picks up a miniature portrait of Lélie after attempting to help the fainted Valère. In The Feast of Stone, this character appears as the servant to Don Juan. This character alternately titles the play The (*) Imaginary Cuckold. While working as a woodcutter, this character is beaten and forced to heal Lucinde in The Doctor in Spite of Himself. The first non-anonymous play to depict the trope of the “flying doctor” casts him as this character. For 10 points, name this recurring Molière character often portrayed by the author himself.
64ab6873f814f28d9b5a7858.question_sanitized: In one play, this character decides upon a change in profession after proclaiming that "a shoe maker in making shoes cannot spoil a scrap of leather without paying for it." After four doctors cannot decide on the best treatment for this character's daughter, he attempts to search for a panacea called Orvietan. This character is ordered by his master to invite a stone statue of "the Commander" in the forest to a dinner. In one play, this character accidentally picks up a miniature portrait of Lelie after attempting to help the fainted Valere. In The Feast of Stone, this character appears as the servant to Don Juan. This character alternately titles the play The (*) Imaginary Cuckold. While working as a woodcutter, this character is beaten and forced to heal Lucinde in The Doctor in Spite of Himself. The first non-anonymous play to depict the trope of the "flying doctor" casts him as this character. For 10 points, name this recurring Moliere character often portrayed by the author himself.
64ab6873f814f28d9b5a7858.answer: Sganarelle [prompt on Molière until mentioned with “what character did he often play?”]
64ab6873f814f28d9b5a7858.answer_sanitized: Sganarelle [prompt on Moliere until mentioned with "what character did he often play?"]
